facts.text: Founded in 1610 as the capital of New Spain, Santa Fe is the oldest state capital in the U.S. and the earliest European settlement west of the Mississippi.
In 1607, Don Pedro de Peralta founded a new city at the foot of the Sangre de Cristo Mountains, naming it La Villa Real de la Santa Fé.
Blending indigenous, Spanish, and American influences, Santa Fe is the cultural capital of the Southwestern United States and a major art city.
Santa Fe is the terminus of three National Historic Trails: El Camino Real de Tierra Adentro, the Old Spanish National Historic Trail, and the Santa Fe National Historic Trail.
Santa Fe's history spans six periods, beginning with the Preconquest era when Pueblo villages occupied the site from about 1050 to 1150.
Since 1926, the Old Santa Fe Association has preserved historic buildings, and a 1958 zoning code keeps the city’s distinctive Spanish‑Pueblo adobe architecture.
